content:
Bill: im not sure what im doing wrong but i dont know how to exaclty get the html to show the diary.i thin now that we are runniing the program on your computer, i need to do something differnt on my computer to get it to load
Tom: You are probably not doing anything wrong. The setup changed. What worked before was UI on your Mac and backend on your Mac, both talking to localhost. What we have now is backend/data on Emily and you are trying to view from your Mac. The UI does not magically know how to reach the Emily service unless we wire that path correctly. The simplest way is: backend on Emily, SSH tunnel from your Mac, UI served locally on your Mac, API base set to http://127.0.0.1:8041.

Bill: yeah, i cant get it to work. please tell me step by step
Tom: Do this exactly. 1. On Emily, start the backend service manually if it is not already running: cd /home/willard/development/agent-diary && PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m agent_diary.cli.main serve --host 127.0.0.1 --port 8041. 2. On your Mac, open a new terminal and create the SSH tunnel: ssh -L 8041:127.0.0.1:8041 emily. 3. On your Mac, open another new terminal and go to your local UI folder: cd /Users/willardmechem/agent-diary/ui && python3 -m http.server 5173. 4. Open http://127.0.0.1:5173 in your browser. 5. In the page, set the API base field to http://127.0.0.1:8041.

Bill: ok. i got it to work. youre so smart tom. first problem i see right away, the only chat thats shown is my writing. your responses are not included
Tom: Yes. That is a real problem, and it is my fault. What I imported was a small chunked reconstruction of today’s interaction, not the full verbatim back-and-forth transcript. So it preserved the themes, but it did not preserve the actual full Tom-side replies the way the real product is supposed to. That means the first guinea-pig import was useful for testing the pipeline, but not yet truthful enough for the product standard we actually want. The right next move is to treat this as a successful pipeline test and then do a second import pass using a much more literal transcript shape where both your messages and my replies are preserved word-for-word inside each imported entry.
